name: Perfect Chocolate Cake (Kh)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine cocoa with boiling water and whisk until smooth. Allow to cool completely.
2. Sift flour, baking soda, salt, and baking powder into a separate bowl.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease three 9-inch round layer cake pans well and lightly dust with flour.
4. In a large bowl of electric mixer, beat butter and sugar at high speed until light, about 5 minutes. Beat in eggs and vanilla, scraping the bowl occasionally, until combined.
5. Gradually add flour mixture in thirds, alternating with cocoa mixture, beginning and ending with flour mixture. Do not overbeat.
6. Divide batter evenly among pans and smooth the top. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until a surface spring back when gently pressed with a fingertip. Allow to cool in pans for 10 minutes. Loosen edges with a spatula and transfer cakes onto racks to cool completely.
7. For frosting: In a medium saucepan, combine chocolate pieces, cream, and butter. Stir over medium heat until smooth. Remove from heat and whisk in confectioners sugar. Set bowl over ice and beat until it holds shape.
8. Prepare filling by whipping cream with sugar and vanilla; refrigerate.
9. Assemble cake: Place a layer on a plate top side down and spread with half of the frosting. Place second layer top side down and spread with remaining frosting. Place third layer top side up.
10. Frost the sides first, covering any exposed whipped cream, then use the rest of the frosting to frost the top, swirling decoratively. Refrigerate at least one hour before serving.
11. To cut, use a thin-edged sharp knife and slice with a sawing motion.
